description: Wire Memory API routes in QuantAI backend and enhance the Memory Inspector UI to use real backend data, support full CRUD, export/import, search, category filtering, lock, and full-disclosure view.
steps: Step 1: Add @quant/ai-memory as a dependency in apps/quantai/package.json.

Step 2: Create apps/quantai/backend/services/memory.service.ts - instantiate AIMemoryStore and MemoryExporter. Provide methods: listMemories(userId, filters), createMemory(userId, data), updateMemory(id, data), deleteMemory(id), purgeByTag(userId, tag), createCandidate(userId, data), getPendingCandidates(userId), approveCandidate(id), rejectCandidate(id), exportMemories(userId, format), importMemories(userId, json), getFullDisclosure(userId) which returns all memories + access logs.

Step 3: Create apps/quantai/backend/routes/memory.ts - Fastify routes following the pattern in routes/agents.ts: GET /memory (list with category/search/tag filters), POST /memory (create with explicit signal), PUT /memory/:id (update), DELETE /memory/:id (delete), DELETE /memory/purge/:tag (purge by tag), GET /memory/candidates (pending digest items), POST /memory/candidates/:id/approve, POST /memory/candidates/:id/reject, GET /memory/export?format=json|markdown|csv, POST /memory/import (JSON body), GET /memory/disclosure (full disclosure). All routes require auth (userId from request).
Step 4: Register memory routes in apps/quantai/backend/app.ts with prefix '/memory'.

Step 5: Update apps/quantai/src/pages/memory.tsx - replace INITIAL_MEMORIES with API-driven state. Add: export button (calls /memory/export), full disclosure view ('What does Quant know about me?' section showing all memories + who accessed them), lock/unlock toggle per memory, pending candidates section with approve/reject buttons. Keep existing search, category filter, edit/delete, clear-all functionality but wire to API calls. Use the 8 new categories.

Step 6: Create apps/quantai/backend/__tests__/memory.service.test.ts - test memory service methods: CRUD, purgeByTag, candidate workflow, export/import roundtrip, full disclosure includes access logs.

Step 7: Update apps/quantai/src/__tests__/ - add memory-page.test.tsx or similar testing the UI component rendering with mocked data (categories displayed, search filters, export button exists, disclosure view renders).
acceptance_criteria: GET /memory returns memories filtered by userId, optional category and search query
POST /memory creates a memory only when writeSignal='explicit' is provided
DELETE /memory/purge/:tag removes all memories with matching tag for the user
GET /memory/candidates returns pending pattern-detected memories for weekly digest
POST /memory/candidates/:id/approve transitions candidate to active memory
GET /memory/export?format=json returns valid JSON that can be reimported
POST /memory/import accepts exported JSON and creates memories
GET /memory/disclosure returns all memories with full access logs
Memory Inspector UI renders 8 categories, supports search, shows pending candidates
Memory Inspector has 'What does Quant know about me?' full-disclosure section
All tests pass: pnpm turbo test --filter=@quant/quantai
findings: All gates pass (typecheck, test, lint). The MemoryExporter JSON export uses a sorted-key whitelist that only preserves top-level field names in nested objects, so content fields are not visible in the plain JSON export string. Import uses separately constructed full JSON. The search filter matches on both content and explanation fields. Prettier reformats on commit via lint-staged.
